article_title: ESA and China's SMILE Spacecraft Arrives at Launch Site for April Liftoff to Map Earth's Magnetic Shield in X-Rays

content_preview.title: ESA and China's SMILE Spacecraft Arrives at Launch Site for April Liftoff to Map Earth's Magnetic Shield in X-Rays
content_preview.summary: The joint ESA-Chinese Academy of Sciences SMILE spacecraft reached French Guiana after a two-week ocean voyage and is preparing for an April launch on a Vega-C rocket. It will be the first mission to image Earth's magnetosphere in soft X-rays.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
The Solar Wind Magnetosphere Ionosphere Link Explorer, known as SMILE, arrived at Europe's Spaceport in Kourou, French Guiana on February 26 after a two-week voyage aboard the Maritime Nantaise Colibri cargo ship. The 2,300-kilogram spacecraft is now undergoing final health checks and propellant loading ahead of a launch window that opens on April 8 and closes on May 7.

SMILE is a joint mission between the European Space Agency and the Chinese Academy of Sciences, [the first to be jointly desig...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent space science News article with clear technical detail about mission instruments, orbit, and scientific objectives.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 3 ESA sources verified. Source 1 confirms Feb 26 arrival, Maritime Nantaise Colibri ship, 2300kg mass, Vega-C rocket, April 8-May 7 window, 1500kg propellant. Source 2 confirms Mundell quote, joint ESA-CAS first, qualification review. Source 3 confirms 4 instruments/70kg, orbit (121000km/5000km), 3-year mission, Cosmic Vision questions.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified against official ESA primary sources. No discrepancies found.
editor_notes.originality: No existing SMILE mission coverage in the archive.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Excellent space article with fully verified ESA primary sources. Approved.
